mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals an imminent labor market deterioration, which reduces consumer spending expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >65% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between retail sales growth and housing starts indicates shifting consumer demand allocation; when retail sales rise while housing starts decline, equity markets price in resilient consumption, generating positive forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative returns in >60% of cases or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index reflects immediate supply-side bottlenecks or demand weakness, triggering short-term risk-off positioning that yields negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >70% of declining INDPRO observations during tightening periods, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation exceeds 3.0%, elevated federal funds rates amplify real discount rate pressures, creating a compounding drag on equity valuations that produces negative forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL > 3.0% and FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and wage growth, which supports corporate revenue projections and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no significant correlation with 60-day forward returns (p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 1.5% annualized signals a contraction in aggregate demand that reduces earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances when GDPC1 growth crosses below the 1.5% threshold, or signal decays in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and rising unemployment confirm a broad-based economic slowdown, triggering immediate algorithmic risk reduction that forces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline condition yields positive 5-day returns in >55% of cases or interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.
